data.title: Douglas Tsao Reiterates Buy on Hansa, Maintains $103 Price Target Amid Strategic Pivot and Operational Recovery
data.description:
Douglas Tsao, an analyst from H.C. Wainwright, reiterated the Buy rating on Hansa Biopharma AB. The associated price target remains the same with SEK103.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Douglas Tsao has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including signs of operational recovery and a clearer strategic focus after the SERB transaction. Second-quarter revenues rebounded sequentially, driven by strong uptake in Spain and continued solid performance in France, Germany, Italy and the UK, reinforcing management’s view that earlier weakness was partly temporary even though near-term volatility is still expected.

The SERB deal provides upfront cash, lowers European cost of goods, and effectively extends Hansa’s financial runway while allowing management to concentrate resources on U.S. approval and launch. With an FDA decision expected in December and imlifidase positioned as a potentially standard-of-care therapy for sensitized kidney transplant patients, Tsao sees substantial long-term value despite near-term reimbursement and economic complexities. His price target remains unchanged at $103, reflecting confidence in the company’s strategic pivot and U.S. growth opportunity.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Patrick Dolezal Reiterates Buy on Surrozen, Keeps Price Target Unchanged at $44 Ahead of Key BRUNELLO Readout
data.description:
LifeSci Capital analyst Patrick Dolezal maintained a Buy rating on Surrozen today and set a price target of $44.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Patrick Dolezal has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including the upcoming BRUNELLO readout of Merck’s MK-3000, which he sees as a key validation event for retinal Wnt agonism and, by extension, Surrozen’s ophthalmology pipeline. He views multiple potential outcome scenarios for BRUNELLO as favorable for Surrozen, particularly those that underscore the need for dual Wnt activation and VEGF inhibition, a profile embodied by Surrozen’s lead asset SZN-8141.

Dolezal considers the main downside risks—either a complete lack of efficacy or unexpected safety issues specifically tied to Wnt agonism—as relatively unlikely, given prior MK-3000 data, Merck’s visible commitment to the program, and the absence of safety-driven trial interruptions. Against this backdrop and with the BRUNELLO data expected in late Q3 or early Q4, he remains constructive on Surrozen’s shares and maintains an unchanged price target of $44, reflecting his confidence in positive read-through to the company’s Wnt-based retinal franchise.

Dolezal covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Neurogene, Oculis Holding, and Ibio. According to TipRanks, Dolezal has an average return of 14.6% and a 42.72% success rate on recommended stocks.

data.title: Ryan Merkel Maintains Hold on Pool as In-Line Results and Mixed Margin Trends Support Balanced Risk-Reward
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Merkel has maintained their neutral stance on POOL stock, giving a Hold rating on July 20.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ryan Merkel has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Pool’s current performance and outlook. The company delivered results and guidance in line with already muted expectations, easing fears that management would sharply cut forecasts, yet not providing enough upside surprise to warrant a more positive stance.

At the same time, cost discipline and SG&A leverage are encouraging, signaling some operational improvement, but pressure on gross margins from higher freight costs and mix remains a concern. With demand trends characterized by steady maintenance activity but sluggish discretionary spending, Merkel sees a balanced risk‑reward profile, supporting a neutral, or Hold, recommendation rather than a more decisive call.

In another report released on July 20, Stifel Nicolaus also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $235.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Maintains Buy on Rollins with Unchanged $67 Price Target Despite Near-Term Margin Pressure
data.description:
Analyst George Tong of Goldman Sachs maintained a Buy rating on Rollins, retaining the price target of $67.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

George Tong has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, even though Rollins’ latest quarter showed softer results versus expectations. He acknowledges that revenue growth decelerated and margins tightened as search-driven lead generation weakened, but he views these pressures as manageable rather than structural, supported by the recent rebound in lead volumes and continued strength in relationship-based channels.

Tong also highlights that core demand in both residential and commercial pest control, as well as termite and ancillary services, remains solid, underpinning Rollins’ long-term growth profile. He expects operational adjustments and cost realignments to help restore profitability as volumes recover, which, combined with the company’s resilient business model, supports maintaining a Buy rating and an unchanged $67 price target.

According to TipRanks, Tong is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 3.6% and a 55.76% success rate. Tong covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Equifax, Cintas, and Korn Ferry.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Fiera as Net Outflows Persist and Price Target Trimmed to $6.50
data.description:
Fiera Capital A, the Financial sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Etienne Ricard from BMO Capital maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a C$6.50 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Etienne Ricard has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Fiera’s recent operating trends and valuation profile. The firm is facing persistent net outflows in both PineStone-related and other public market strategies, and the latest quarter showed weaker-than-expected assets under management, leading him to trim his target price from $7.00 to $6.50.

At the same time, Fiera’s shares trade at a relatively attractive free cash flow yield and the company benefits from diversified capabilities across public and private markets, which anchor downside risk and leave room for upside if flows improve. Ricard’s Hold stance reflects his view that a meaningful and sustained recovery in net inflows is necessary before the stock’s valuation can re-rate closer to peers, justifying a neutral recommendation for now.

In another report released on July 13, RBC Capital also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a C$6.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
